response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished, craftsmanship-oriented engineer with strong distributed-systems, high-performance, and event-driven experience, but the role centers on C++/STL development and industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443, SDL), which are barely represented. C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments around 2014–2015 plus an academic thesis; the primary modern stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. There is no evidence of MQTT, message brokers, industrial automation protocols, or secure-development-lifecycle work, leaving the core requirements largely unmet despite a strong general engineering profile.
strengths: Proven high-performance, multi-threaded, networked systems work (millions of req/sec, async I/O, fibers)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, mentoring and teaching
Broad polyglot background with event-driven architecture and some embedded/robotics exposure
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months) and roughly a decade old; not the current stack
No IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or industrial cybersecurity background
No demonstrated MQTT, message-broker, or industrial-automation protocol experience
